NCT02677896.name: ARCHES
NCT02677896.group: mHSPC, enzalutamide + ADT vs placebo + ADT
NCT02677896.snippet: Source: Armstrong AJ et al. JCO 2019;37:2974-2986 (ARCHES); Armstrong JCO 2022 (OS final).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 1,150 men with mHSPC randomized 1:1 to enzalutamide 160 mg daily + ADT (n=574) vs placebo + ADT (n=576). Prior docetaxel was permitted (17.7% received).

evidence.label: Primary — Radiographic PFS (2019)
evidence.text: Median rPFS not reached with enzalutamide vs 19.0 months placebo. HR 0.39 (95% CI 0.30-0.50; P<0.001). Consistent benefit across pre-specified subgroups.

evidence.label: Overall Survival Update
evidence.text: Final OS analysis: median OS not reached enzalutamide vs not reached placebo; HR 0.66 (95% CI 0.53-0.81; P<0.001). Benefit maintained across high- and low-volume disease subgroups.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Subject is considered an adult according to local regulation at the time of signing informed consent.~* Subject is diagnosed with histologically or cytologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the prostate without neuroendocrine differentiation, signet cell or small cell histology.~* Subject has metastatic prostate cancer documented by positive bone scan (for bone disease) or metastatic lesions on computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scan (for soft tis...

NCT02446405.name: ENZAMET
NCT02446405.group: mHSPC, enzalutamide vs standard nonsteroidal AR antagonist
NCT02446405.snippet: Source: Davis ID et al. NEJM 2019;381:121-131 (ENZAMET); 5-yr update Sweeney et al. Lancet Oncol 2023.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 1,125 men with mHSPC randomized 1:1 to enzalutamide (n=562) vs standard nonsteroidal antiandrogen (bicalutamide/nilutamide/flutamide) (n=563) added to testosterone suppression. Concurrent docetaxel allowed (45% received).

evidence.label: Primary — Overall Survival
evidence.source: Davis ID et al. NEJM 2019, Figure 2
evidence.text: At 3 years, OS 80% enzalutamide vs 72% standard. HR 0.67 (95% CI 0.52-0.86; P=0.002). Benefit evident across volume of disease and docetaxel strata.

evidence.label: 5-year Follow-up (2023)
evidence.source: Sweeney CJ et al. Lancet Oncol 2023 (ENZAMET 5y)
evidence.text: 5-year OS: 67% enzalutamide vs 57% standard; HR 0.70 (0.58-0.84). Durable OS benefit maintained.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Men starting first line androgen deprivation therapy for metastatic prostate cancer.~Inclusion criteria:~1. Male aged 18 or older with metastatic adenocarcinoma of the prostate~2. Target or non-target lesions according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumours (RECIST) 1.1~3. Adequate bone marrow function: Haemoglobin (Hb) ≥100g/L and White Cell Count (WCC) ≥ 4.0 x 109/L and platelets ≥100 x 109/L.~4. Adequate liver function: Alanine transaminase (ALT) < 2 x Upper Limit of Normal (ULN) ...

NCT02489318.name: TITAN
NCT02489318.group: mHSPC, apalutamide + ADT vs placebo + ADT
NCT02489318.snippet: Source: Chi KN et al. NEJM 2019;381:13-24 (TITAN); final OS Chi JCO 2021.

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 1,052 men with mHSPC randomized 1:1 to apalutamide 240 mg + ADT (n=525) vs placebo + ADT (n=527). Prior docetaxel permitted (10.7% received).

evidence.label: Co-Primary — Overall Survival and rPFS
evidence.source: Chi KN et al. NEJM 2019 + JCO 2021
evidence.text: At first interim (2019): rPFS HR 0.48 (0.39-0.60; P<0.001); OS HR 0.67 (0.51-0.89). Final analysis (JCO 2021) with crossover-adjusted OS HR 0.65 (0.53-0.79); 39% relative reduction in risk of death.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Diagnosis of prostate adenocarcinoma as confirmed by the investigator~* Metastatic disease documented by greater than or equal to (>=) 1 bone lesions on 99mTc bone scan. Participants with a single bone lesion must have confirmation of bone metastasis by computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)~* Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status (ECOG PS) grade of 0 or 1~* Participants who received docetaxel treatment must meet the following criteri...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Race: American Indian or Alaska Native 11, American Indian or Alaska Native 6, American Indian or Alaska Native 17, Asian 112, Asian 119. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NCT02799602.name: ARASENS
NCT02799602.group: mHSPC, darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT vs placebo + docetaxel + ADT (triplet)
NCT02799602.snippet: Source: Smith MR et al. NEJM 2022;386:1132-1142 (ARASENS).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.text: 1,306 men with mHSPC (all fit for docetaxel) randomized 1:1 to darolutamide 600 mg BID + docetaxel 75 mg/m^2 (6 cycles) + ADT (n=651) vs placebo + docetaxel + ADT (n=654). All patients received triplet intensification.

evidence.label: Primary — Overall Survival
evidence.source: Smith MR et al. NEJM 2022, Figure 2
evidence.text: Median OS not reached darolutamide-triplet vs 48.9 months placebo-triplet. HR 0.68 (95% CI 0.57-0.80; P<0.001). 32% relative reduction in risk of death.

evidence.label: Context — Triplet vs Doublet
evidence.source: Smith MR et al. NEJM 2022 Editorial
evidence.text: ARASENS uses triplet comparator; the enzalutamide/apalutamide trials (ARCHES/ENZAMET/TITAN) compared ARPI+ADT vs ADT. When pooling, subgroup by triplet vs doublet is essential. ARASENS supports adding ARPI to docetaxel+ADT backbone.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Histologically or cytologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of prostate.~* Metastatic disease~* Candidates for ADT and docetaxel.~* Started ADT with or without first generation anti androgen, but no longer than 12 weeks before randomization~* An Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 or 1~* Adequate bone marrow, liver and renal function~Exclusion Criteria:~* Prior treatment with: LHRH agonist/antagonists; second generation androgen receptor (AR) inh...

NCT01715285.name: LATITUDE
NCT01715285.group: High-risk mHSPC, abiraterone + prednisone + ADT vs placebo + ADT (overall survival HR)

allOutcomes.title: Overall survival (primary, abiraterone+ADT vs placebo+ADT; LATITUDE is the original modern ARPI-mHSPC landmark)

NCT01715285.snippet: Source: Fizazi K et al. NEJM 2017;377:352-360 (LATITUDE). Landmark abiraterone-in-mHSPC pivotal.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: MALE; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Newly diagnosed metastatic prostate cancer within 3 months prior to randomization with histologically or cytologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the prostate without neuroendocrine differentiation or small cell histology~* Distant metastatic disease documented by positive bone scan or metastatic lesions on computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scan~* At least 2 of the following high-risk prognostic factors: Gleason score of greater than or equal to...

title: RapidMeta Oncology | AR Pathway Inhibitors in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer v1.0
